findings.summary: Settings class in common.config imports REFUSAL_PHRASES from common.constants, embedding OCR-domain knowledge in the shared configuration layer.
evidence: from .constants import REFUSAL_PHRASES used to build OCR_REFUSAL_MARKERS default
findings.suggestion: Move refusal phrase defaults into the OCR package or make them purely configurable via environment variables.

findings.summary: OcrProcessor vs ClassificationProcessor creates asymmetric naming (abbreviated vs full form) for the same role across packages.
findings.suggestion: Use consistently abbreviated (OcrProcessor/ClassifierProcessor) or full form (OcrProcessor/ClassificationProcessor). Current names are acceptable but asymmetric.

findings.summary: OcrProvider catches openai.APIError directly while ClassificationProvider uses _create_with_compat with separate BadRequestError handling, creating inconsistent error handling.
findings.suggestion: Document why the OCR path intentionally skips compatibility parameter stripping, or align the error handling patterns.

findings.summary: Three module-level functions (init_openai_client, get_openai_client, is_openai_client_ready) delegate to _openai_holder methods with no added logic.
findings.suggestion: Expose the holder instance directly or pass the client as a constructor parameter to providers.

findings.summary: Both httpx and openai SDK bundle httpx internally, creating implicit version coupling.
evidence: httpx~=0.28 and openai~=1.35 both in dependencies
findings.suggestion: Verify httpx version range stays compatible with the openai SDK's internal httpx dependency.

findings.summary: Every test in test_worker.py patches claim_processing_tag and release_processing_tag at module level, coupling tests to import paths.
findings.suggestion: Consider injecting claim/release as constructor parameters or using a fixture to reduce per-test patching boilerplate.

findings.summary: Private helper functions like _split_footer, _extract_page_numbers have verbose reStructuredText docstrings for implementation details.
evidence: def _split_footer(content: str) -> tuple[str, str]: multi-line reST docstring
findings.suggestion: Use concise one-line docstrings for private helpers; reserve detailed docstrings for public API.

findings.summary: classifier.quality_gates imports REFUSAL_PHRASES directly from common.constants rather than using Settings.
findings.suggestion: Receive refusal phrases via Settings or a classifier-local constant.

findings.summary: OcrProcessor and ClassificationProcessor share identical claim/release/stats pattern but no shared base class.
evidence: Both classes: init with doc/client/provider/settings, process() with claim/finally/release/log-stats
findings.suggestion: Consider extracting a base ProcessorTemplate for the common orchestration pattern.

findings.summary: TaxonomyCache._get_or_create_item_id calls self.refresh() inside the RLock, performing HTTP calls while holding the lock.
evidence: with self._lock: ... self.refresh() performs three API calls
findings.suggestion: Release the lock before refreshing or use a read-write lock pattern.

findings.summary: In truncate_content_by_pages, preamble text before the first page header is silently included but not counted as a page.
claim_processing_tag performs a non-atomic read-modify-write-verify sequence without true locking, allowing a race between concurrent workers.
evidence: Check tag_id not in current_tags, update, then verify — race between check and update
findings.suggestion: Document the race window explicitly and note that verify-after-write provides best-effort protection.
